after: yah-kg-daemon test `reindex_after_disk_delete_wipes_file_nodes` is pre-existing red on macOS — relativize() canonicalize-fallback when the file is gone makes reindex_path early-return. Unrelated to this pass.

before: Pass 1 done: AnnotationKind::Relay(WorkItemAnno) + Ticket(WorkItemAnno) variants in yah-kg/src/anno.rs, with full payload (id, title, kind, status, assignee, parent, phase, severity, handoff[], next_steps[], gotchas[], assumes[], verify[], cleanup[]). TicketStatus enum (Open/Claimed/InProgress/Handoff/Review/Done) with kebab-case parse + as_str. yah-kg-anno parser extended: RawAnnotation::WorkItem variant + WorkItemBuilder accumulator that groups @yah:relay/@yah:ticket headers with their modifier directives (status/assignee/parent/phase/severity/kind/handoff/next/gotcha/assumes/verify/cleanup); blank lines or back-to-back headers close blocks. Applier writes WorkItem annos into AnnotationIndex. Tests: 8 new unit tests + 1 e2e test (relay_and_ticket_round_trip_through_annotation_index) — all 27 yah-kg-anno tests pass; full workspace builds clean (yah-tauri exclusion is the pre-existing R014-T2 missing-icon issue, unrelated).
after: Pass 3 RPC + orphan-GC stretch both landed. arch.list_tickets / arch.list_relays / arch.get_ticket served from KgService and the Tauri command layer; reads walk incoming Anchors. apply_pass now ends with gc_orphan_synthetics() — fixpoint sweep that removes synthetic Tag nodes with no incoming Tag edge and Relay/Ticket nodes with no Anchors AND no incoming ParentItem (stub parents stay alive while children reference them; cascade-removal of a child orphans its parent stub on the next iteration). ApplySummary.orphans_gced for observability. yah-kg-anno 16/16 incl. 2 new sweep tests; yah-kg-daemon 12/13 (macOS gotcha).

before: Add Relay + Ticket variants to the annotation enum (status, kind, handoff, gotchas)
Store as their own node kinds (CommonKind::Relay/::Ticket or new variant)
Watcher re-emits ticket-changed events on save so the Board UI updates live

Pass 2: promote to first-class graph citizens. Add CommonKind::Relay + CommonKind::Ticket synthetic node kinds in yah-kg/src/kind.rs (parallel to CommonKind::Tag). Applier in yah-kg-anno upserts a synthetic node per work item (id format: 'relay:R042' / 'ticket:R042-T1'), adds Parent edges from ticket→relay and relay→relay, optionally DependsOn edges if depends_on field is added later. Watcher emits dedicated TicketChanged/RelayChanged events through the event_bridge so the Tauri UI can subscribe to ticket-only changes instead of generic AnnotationChanged. Then wire RPC: arch_list_tickets, arch_get_ticket(id), and the mutation surface (R017-F5).

Open question for pass 2: should @yah:relay/ticket headers attach to the structural anchor (current behavior — module/struct/fn) AND a synthetic ticket node, or only the synthetic node? Current pass 1 attaches to the structural anchor — pass 2 needs to decide whether the synthetic node is canonical or shadow.
after: Pass 3 cleanup — unify yah/src/arch/ticket.rs (Ticket/TicketBoard, ~2.4kloc) with yah_kg::anno::WorkItemAnno + WorkItem. Recommendation: keep computed fields (lex-first winner, FieldConflict, is_epic, epic_status, ChildLiveCounts) on a yah-board recompute layer that consumes Vec<WorkItem> from arch.list_*; they're presentation concerns (epic inference walks the whole board, FieldConflict needs cross-anchor scalar comparison) and don't belong on the wire DTO.
Frontend swap to arch_list_tickets/arch_list_relays/arch_get_ticket once unification lands, then archive R017-F4.

Migrate yah/src/arch/ticket.rs (Ticket/TicketBoard) onto yah_kg::Board. Add yah-kg as a path dep on yah/Cargo.toml. The current build_item folds &[ArchAnnotation] into a Ticket; replace with an in-process adapter that emits Vec<WorkItem> from the CLI's parsed annotations, then call Board::from_work_items. Computed fields (is_epic / epic_status / conflicts) are no longer fields on Ticket — they live on BoardItem. The flat Ticket.file / .line / .target convenience aliases stay for back-compat (read from BoardItem.item.anchors[0]).
Once the CLI swap lands, re-target the hack-board frontend to arch.list_tickets / arch.list_relays / arch.get_ticket (already served by the daemon) and archive R017-F4.

Per-file fold_file + PartialTicket survive; build_item/merge_scalar/record_conflict/extend_dedup/resolve_epics/compute_epic_status are gone. ticket.title: Skip @yah: annotations inside backtick/code-fence spans in doc comments
KG features: validator + snapshot persistence + relay/ticket nodes
yah-kg-validator crate + vocabulary (no-import-of, no-dependency-on, max-depth, must-tag)
next_steps: Walks KG, applies rules from index, returns Vec<Violation>
Cargo.toml [workspace.metadata.arch] section declares legal rule kinds + tag namespaces
Parser already handles @yah:rule(no-import-of: tag(view)); the type exists in the contract — wire validation

ticket.title: arch.validate(scope) RPC + Tauri command surface
next_steps: Tauri command surfaces violations to the UI for inline display

ticket.title: KgService::save(path) + ::load(path) snapshots; mtime-diff on boot
next_steps: Use postcard for size; serde_json acceptable as a v1
Write snapshot on IndexFinished, replay on next boot, then quick mtime-diff to bring stale files current
Old rs-hack-arch had source_hash caching worth lifting
verify: Cold boot on a workspace with a saved snapshot is order-of-magnitude faster than full reindex

ticket.title: Tauri commands for ticket mutation paths (move ticket → status rewrites source comment)
next_steps: Mutation rewrites the @yah:status(...) line in source; watcher re-indexes; UI gets event

after: Backend surface for arch.validate landed. yah-kg now owns the wire types in a new src/validate.rs (Scope, Severity, Violation — Scope uses tagged-struct variants {scope:'all'|'subtree'|'file'} so the JSON shape is self-describing); yah-kg-validator re-exports them via its existing names so engine/tests stayed compatible (engine destructures the new struct variants; tests/engine.rs::scope_file_filters_anchors updated to Scope::File { path: ... }). yah-kg::rpc gained ValidateParams { #[serde(default)] scope: Scope } + ValidateResult { violations: Vec<Violation> } + method::VALIDATE constant + RpcRequest/RpcResponse variants. yah-kg-daemon depends on yah-kg-validator and exposes KgService::validate(params) -> ValidateResult that takes read locks on store+annotations and runs the validator. app/tauri/src/commands.rs adds arch_validate(rig_id, params: Option<ValidateParams>) -> Result<ValidateResult, String> (Option mirrors arch_list_tickets/arch_list_relays so the renderer can call invoke('arch_validate', { rig_id }) and get a Scope::All run by default). Registered in app/tauri/src/lib.rs invoke_handler. cargo build -p yah-tauri green; cargo test -p yah-kg -p yah-kg-validator -p yah-kg-anno -p yah-kg-daemon 11/11 + 19/19 + e2e all green. Pre-existing yah test arch::mcp::tests::test_tool_definitions (asserts len==7 against a list of 6) is unrelated breakage from prior commits.

before: Tauri command surfaces violations to the UI for inline display
after: Tauri command surfaces violations to the UI for inline display

yah-ui: add rpc.validate(rigId, scope?) -> ValidateResult to env/index.ts + tauri.ts (invoke('arch_validate', { rigId, params: scope ? { scope } : undefined })); browser.ts can return { violations: [] } as a stub
ArchView: call rpc.validate(rigId) on mount + after every IndexFinished event; surface violations as inline pills/badges on the offending nodes (use Violation.offending → graph node)
TicketCard / Board: also fetch + show violations whose anchor is in the current rig — the rule lives on a structural node but reads as a status line on the relay/ticket that owns it
Add 'arch.validate' to yah-ui/src/types.ts wire mirrors (WireViolation, WireSeverity, WireScope) so the env adapter is fully typed

before: Passes 1–5 of R017-F4 landed: WorkItemAnno parser + synthetic Relay/Ticket nodes (Pass 1–2), arch.list_tickets/list_relays/get_ticket RPC + orphan-GC sweep (Pass 3), yah-kg::board recompute layer (Pass 4), and now the CLI swap (Pass 5) — yah/src/arch/ticket.rs's TicketBoard::from_annotations delegates cross-anchor recompute (epic inference, scalar conflicts) to yah_kg::board::Board::from_work_items. Per-file fold_file + PartialTicket survive; build_item/merge_scalar/record_conflict/extend_dedup/resolve_epics/compute_epic_status are gone. A Sidecar carries depends_on/see_also/target and the cross-anchor vec union (handoff/next/cleanup/verify/gotchas/assumes) since Board only reads anchors[0].anno from the wire DTO. yah-kg added as a path dep on yah/Cargo.toml. yah lib 147/147, arch_dogfood 26/26, arch_non_rust_extract 10/10, yah-kg 20/20, yah-kg-anno 19/19, yah-kg-daemon e2e 12/13 (macOS gotcha unchanged); yah board show/status dogfood clean. Remaining: hack-board frontend swap onto arch.list_tickets / arch.list_relays / arch.get_ticket — Tauri-track agent owns it.
after: Pass 3 RPC + orphan-GC stretch both landed. arch.list_tickets / arch.list_relays / arch.get_ticket served from KgService and the Tauri command layer; reads walk incoming Anchors. apply_pass now ends with gc_orphan_synthetics() — fixpoint sweep that removes synthetic Tag nodes with no incoming Tag edge and Relay/Ticket nodes with no Anchors AND no incoming ParentItem (stub parents stay alive while children reference them; cascade-removal of a child orphans its parent stub on the next iteration). ApplySummary.orphans_gced for observability. yah-kg-anno 16/16 incl. 2 new sweep tests; yah-kg-daemon 12/13 (macOS gotcha).

before: Re-target the hack-board frontend to arch.list_tickets / arch.list_relays / arch.get_ticket — owned by the yah-ui / Tauri-as-server track. After the frontend lands, archive R017-F4.
after: Pass 3 cleanup — unify yah/src/arch/ticket.rs (Ticket/TicketBoard, ~2.4kloc) with yah_kg::anno::WorkItemAnno + WorkItem. Recommendation: keep computed fields (lex-first winner, FieldConflict, is_epic, epic_status, ChildLiveCounts) on a yah-board recompute layer that consumes Vec<WorkItem> from arch.list_*; they're presentation concerns (epic inference walks the whole board, FieldConflict needs cross-anchor scalar comparison) and don't belong on the wire DTO.
Frontend swap to arch_list_tickets/arch_list_relays/arch_get_ticket once unification lands, then archive R017-F4.

ticket.title: Skip @yah: annotations inside backtick/code-fence spans in doc comments

after: KG snapshot persistence landed. New surface: KgService::save(path) / load(path) / boot_with_snapshot(rig_root, snapshot_path) / save_default() — backed by KgSnapshot { rig_root, fingerprints, store, annotations } at .yah/cache/snapshot.json (v1, atomic temp+rename, JSON for v1 — postcard slot reserved by SNAPSHOT_VERSION). yah-kg-store now exposes Store::to_snapshot/restore + StoreSnapshot/SnapshotError; yah-kg-anno exposes AnnotationIndex::to_snapshot/restore + AnnotationIndexSnapshot. yah-kg-daemon got snapshot.rs (FileFingerprint mtime+size, fingerprint_rig walker mirroring walk_and_index skip rules, diff_fingerprints → ReconcilePlan { unchanged/changed/removed }). boot() now refreshes fingerprints; boot_with_snapshot loads + walks + reconciles via reindex_path on each changed/removed file (and falls back to a full boot when the snapshot is missing or rig_root mismatched). Tests: 5 new e2e (round-trip, fallback, mtime-diff skip-vs-reindex, deletion reconcile, rig_root mismatch), 21/21 daemon e2e green; yah-kg / yah-kg-store / yah-kg-anno / yah-kg-validator unit tests all green; yah-tauri builds. Source-hash caching from old rs-hack-arch is the next optimization layer (currently mtime+size only).

before: Use postcard for size; serde_json acceptable as a v1
Write snapshot on IndexFinished, replay on next boot, then quick mtime-diff to bring stale files current
Old rs-hack-arch had source_hash caching worth lifting
after: Use postcard for size; serde_json acceptable as a v1
Write snapshot on IndexFinished, replay on next boot, then quick mtime-diff to bring stale files current
Old rs-hack-arch had source_hash caching worth lifting

Wire boot_with_snapshot into the Tauri host's startup path: app/tauri/src/lib.rs sets up a KgService per rig — replace boot() with boot_with_snapshot(rig_root, default_snapshot_path(rig_root)). Add a save() call after IndexFinished in the Tauri command layer (or on app suspend) so subsequent cold-starts pay near-zero cost.
Optional: add a yahd CLI 'snapshot save/show' subcommand for manual cache regen + inspection — useful for the verify gate ('order-of-magnitude faster') in measurable form.
Optional: lift source_hash caching from the old rs-hack-arch — fingerprint contents not just mtime+size, so an 'unchanged' file with a fresh mtime (touch, git checkout) is correctly skipped.

Verify the order-of-magnitude speedup gate from R017-F3 against a real-sized rig (rs-hack itself is fine)

Tauri ticket-move surface landed. New shared module yah-kg/src/board_mutate.rs (pure-string) owns the transition matrix (bucket_to_status, status_to_bucket, allowed_transitions) and the source-rewrite helpers (locate_ticket_block, set_or_insert_annotation, comment_sigil) — same matrix the existing yah board move CLI and hack-board TS server enforce, but now callable from a library rather than only from the yah binary. KgService::move_ticket(MoveTicketParams { id, to_bucket }) -> MoveTicketResult { id, from_status, to_status, file, line } in yah-kg-daemon: takes read locks, derives epic-ness via Board::from_work_items, walks the lex-first anchor, validates transition, rewrites source, and calls reindex_path with IndexReason::AgentEdit so subscribers see one WorkItemChanged event with the right cause. Domain rejections surface as new DaemonError::Conflict(String) — analogous to the board server's HTTP 409. Tauri command arch_move_ticket(rig_id, params) is a thin wrapper, registered in app/tauri/src/lib.rs invoke_handler. Tests: 7 unit tests in board_mutate (transitions, bucket round-trip, rewrite in //!/// /# blocks, multi-id isolation, missing-id) + 4 new e2e tests (happy path with AgentEdit event assertion, disallowed transition, unknown bucket / missing id, epic refusal). cargo test -p yah-kg 33/33, cargo test -p yah-kg-daemon 26/26, cargo build -p yah-tauri green.

before: Mutation rewrites the @yah:status(...) line in source; watcher re-indexes; UI gets event
Cover the same transition matrix as the existing board server (open→active, active→{open,handoff,review}, handoff→{active,review}, review→handoff)
after: Mutation rewrites the @yah:status(...) line in source; watcher re-indexes; UI gets event
Cover the same transition matrix as the existing board server (open→active, active→{open,handoff,review}, handoff→{active,review}, review→handoff)

yah-ui: add rpc.move_ticket(rigId, id, to_bucket) -> MoveTicketResult to env/index.ts + tauri.ts (invoke('arch_move_ticket', { rigId, params: { id, toBucket } })); browser.ts can throw 'not implemented' as a stub

BoardView drag-and-drop: call rpc.move_ticket on column drop; show the Conflict error message as a toast on rejection (epic mutation, transition denied) — these aren't bugs, they're the same UI dim semantics the existing hack-board server returns

Cleanup: yah/src/main.rs handle_move() still has its own copies of bucket_to_status, status_to_bucket, allowed_transitions, locate_ticket_block, set_or_insert_annotation, comment_sigil — fold them onto yah_kg::board_mutate so the matrix lives in exactly one place (currently lives in 2: this new module + yah/src/main.rs; the TS copy in hack-board/src/server.ts is a separate language so it'll always be a parallel mirror)

Cleanup: rich payload mutations (handoff/next/verify/cleanup/gotcha/assumes append) are still CLI-only — handle_move accepts them via flags but the Tauri move_ticket only handles status. Add a separate arch_append_annotation command if/when the UI grows handoff/next-step authoring affordances
